title: The One After Ross Says Rachel

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: Friends. Family. We are gathered to celebrate here today the joyous union of Ross and Emily. Now Ross, repeat after me. I Ross...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I Ross...

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: Take thee, Emily...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Take thee, Rachel... Emily. Emily.
lines.stage_direction: All his friends have looks of shock on their faces. He realizes what he said. Quickly he says. A slight chuckle.

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: Uhh...Shall I go on?
lines.stage_direction: Looking and feeling awkward. he looks towards Emily.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He-he said Rachel, right? Do you think I should go up there?
lines.stage_direction: To the woman sitting in front of her

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Yes, yes, do go on.

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: I think we’d better start again. Ross, repeat after me. I, Ross…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I, Ross…

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: Take thee, EM-I-LY …

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Take thee, Emily . Like there’d be anybody else.
lines.stage_direction: Glares at the Minister Chuckles Emily is glaring at him.

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: As my lawfully wedded wife, in sickness and in health, till death parts us.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: As my lawfully wedded wife, in sickness and in health, until death parts us. Really, I do. Emily.

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: May I have the rings? Emily, place this ring on Ross’s finger as a symbol of your bond everlasting. Ross, place this ring in Emily’s hand as a symbol of the love that encircles you forever.
lines.stage_direction: He is given the rings She jams the ring onto his finger

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Happy too.

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: Ross and Emily have made their declarations and it gives me great pleasure to declare them husband and wife.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yay!

lines.speaker: Minister
lines.text: You may kiss the bride.

scenes.scene_description: Ross’s Wedding, continued from last season, the Minister is about to marry Ross and Emily.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: This is worse than when he married the lesbian.

scenes.scene_description: He goes to kiss her, but she isn’t very receptive of the kiss. She keeps avoiding him, until Ross finally gets to kiss her on her cheek.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Just keep smiling.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, that went well. Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It could’ve been worse, he could’ve shot her.

scenes.scene_description: The band starts to play, and the recessional starts. Ross tries to take Emily’s hand, but she snatches it away from him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That uh, that was pretty funny. Wasn’t it?

scenes.scene_description: Ross and Emily make it to the lobby.
Emily gives him a forearm shot across the stomach.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: You’ve spoiled everything! It’s like a nightmare! My friends and family are out there! How can I face them?! How can you do this to me?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, no matter what happens with Ross and Emily, we still get cake right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That-that-that’s all right, no honey, you take your time sweetie. I’ll be right out here. She’s just fixing her makeup.
lines.stage_direction: exiting the bathroom She slams the door in his face, to the gang

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I hate you!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And, I love you!!
lines.stage_direction: He walks into the living room

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Boy, bad time to say the wrong name, huh Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That’s true, thanks dad. People should be dancing! Huh? Hey, this is a party! Come on! Joey, dance!!
lines.stage_direction: To All He starts to dance but stops when no one else joins him.

scenes.scene_description: The Wedding reception, Ross and Emily are in the bathroom and Emily is yelling at him. Rachel, Chandler, Joey, and Monica are standing outside the doorway.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: Yes, Waltham interiors.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh, hello, this is Ross Geller’s personal physician, Dr. Philange.
lines.stage_direction: On the phone, in New York

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I’ve discovered that Ross forgot to take his brain medicine, uh, now without it, uh, in the brain of Ross, uh women’s names are interchangeable, through-through no fault of his own.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: Oh my God, Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, not Phoebe, Dr. Philange. Oh no! You have it too!

scenes.scene_description: Mrs. Waltham’s phone rings and she answers it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hello?

scenes.scene_description: Mrs. Waltham hangs up on her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh wow, I hope you don’t take this the wrong way but, I know we had plans to meet up tonight and, ugh, I’m just kinda worried about what it might do to our friendship.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know. How could we have let this happen?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Seven times!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ugh! Well, y’know, we were away…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: In a foreign , romantic country…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I blame London.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bad London!
lines.stage_direction: Takes a spoon and smacks the turkey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So look umm, while we’re st-still in London, I mean, we can keep doing it right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I don’t see that we have a choice. But, when we’re back home, we don’t do it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Only here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y’know, I saw a wine cellar downstairs…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’ll meet you there in two minutes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay!

scenes.scene_description: Cut to Chandler and Monica at the buffet table.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mon, honey, I gotta ask you something.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Now?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross said my name up there, I mean, come on, I just can’t pretend that didn’t happen can I?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I-I don’t know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica, what should I do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Just uh, do the right thing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Toe the line. Thread the needle. Think outside the box!
lines.stage_direction: Tries to leave, but is stopped by Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whoa, wait, listen, I think I’m just gonna talk to Ross about what he think it meant.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait. Rachel, no, he’s married. Married! If you don’t realize that, I can’t help you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, you’re right. You’re right. You can’t help me.

scenes.scene_description: He throws down his plate and runs to the wine cellar, Monica is about to follow him but is intercepted by Rachel.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Jack, is it all our fault? Were we bad parents?

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Oh yeah, well who serves steak when there’s no place to sit, I mean how are you supposed to eat this?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, what’s up?
lines.stage_direction: He has solved the problem of eating the steak, he’s eating it with his hands.

scenes.scene_description: Cut to Mr. and Mrs. Geller.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Where were you? We were supposed to meet in the wine cellar?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Forget it, that’s off.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why?!

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: The next tour of the wine cellar will plan in two in-in minutes…

scenes.scene_description: Cut to Monica and Chandler, Monica is running up to him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, what are you doing? You promised Phoebe you wouldn’t eat meat until she has the babies!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I figured we’re in another country, so it doesn’t count.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s true.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The man’s got a point.

scenes.scene_description: Joey walks up to them.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi. Sorry, things aren’t working out so well.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh no! It could be better, but it’s gonna be okay, right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah! Of course, I mean, she’s gonna get over this, y’know? I mean, so you said my name! Y’know you just said it ‘cause you saw me there, if you’d have seen a circus freak, you would’ve said, "I take thee circus freak." Y’know, it didn’t mean anything, it’s just a mistake. It didn’t mean anything. Right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No! Of course it didn’t mean anything! I mean, uh well, I can understand why Emily would think it meant something, y’know, because-because it was you…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Right…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But it absolutely didn’t. It didn’t!! It didn’t!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross, hey, the band’s ready outside for your first dance with Emily, so…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! Oh-oh, the band’s ready! Well, I-I-we gotta do what the band says—I don’t care about the stupid band!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You spit on me man!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I’m sorry.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Emily is kinda taking a long time, huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y’know when I locked myself in the bathroom at my wedding, it was because I was trying to pop the window out of the frame.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, right!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Get the hell out of there, y’know?

scenes.scene_description: Cut to Rachel and Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Emily? Emily? I’m coming in.
lines.stage_direction: Bangs on the bathroom door He opens the door to reveal that the window is gone, along with Emily.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, look at that, same thing.

scenes.scene_description: They all start laughing, and quickly stop when they realize what she just said and run over to the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Listen, in the middle of everything if I scream the word, "Yippee!" just ignore me.

scenes.scene_description: London Marriott , Monica and Chandler are walking to her room.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, Rachel! Hi!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, hello Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross said my name. Okay? My name. Ross said my name up there that obviously means that he still loves me! Okay, don’t believe me, I know I’m right—do you guys want to go downstairs and get a drink?
lines.stage_direction: They both just stare at her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, we do. But, we have to change first.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes, I want to change. And why-why don’t you go down and get us a table?

lines.text: Yeah, we’ll be down in like five minutes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Fifteen minutes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.text: Hello? Oh, Pheebs! It’s Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, yay…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Great…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi, so what happened?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, Ross said my name.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I know, but I don’t think that means anything.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, Pheebs, y’know what, let’s look at this objectively all right? Ninth grade, right? The obsession starts. All right? The summer after ninth grade he sees me in a two-piece for the first time, his obsession begins to grow. So then…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, listen, why don’t we go change in my room?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But my clothes are—ohh!

scenes.scene_description: The phone rings and Rachel answers it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow, you look…

lines.speaker: Monica

lines.text: Well, I’ve got a girl in here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, we’re-we’re hanging out in here!

lines.text: Well, I suppose I’d have to say you!! But, what if we’re watching a movie in here?

lines.text: Which we are, and-and we already paid for it. It’s My Giant !

lines.text: My Giant ? I love that movie!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You really think this is okay?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, Ross and Emily aren’t gonna use it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, it’s so beautiful. Ohh! Y’know, I-I don’t know if I feel right about this.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh Mon-Mon-Mon-Mon-look, this is the honeymoon suite. The room expects sex. The room would be disappointed if it didn’t get sex. All of the other honeymoon suites would think it was a loser.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay!

lines.text: Emily?!

lines.text: No! For all I know, she’s trying to find me but couldn’t because I kept moving around. No, from now on, I’m staying in one place. Right here.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, it’s getting late.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, we’re gonna go.

lines.speaker: Ross

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ugh, y’know, umm we gotta get up early and catch that plane for New York.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, it’s a very large plane.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That’s cool.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But, we’ll stay here with you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks guys! I really appreciate this, y’know, but you don’t have to rub my butt.

scenes.scene_description: They both run to rip the covers off the bed, but are interrupted by Ross.
Chandler slowly takes his hand away.

lines.text: We have to leave for New York in an hour.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know, I’ve been looking at those doors, they look pretty sound proof, don’t you think?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We can’t do that that’s insane. I mean ‘A’ he could wake up and ‘B’ y’know, let’s go for it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Em-Emily? Em-Emily? Emily!
lines.stage_direction: Looking around for her. He runs to the door. He opens the door to reveal the Walthams standing outside.

lines.text: No.

lines.text: You can forget about Emily, she’s not with us.

lines.text: We’ve come for her things.

lines.text: Wait, well wh-wh-wh-where is she?

lines.text: She’s in hiding. She’s utterly humiliated. She doesn’t want to see you ever again.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: We’re very sad that it didn’t work out between you and Emily, monkey. But, I think you’re absolutely delicious.

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: Excuse me, I’m standing right here!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: Oh yes, there you are.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey-hey, you guys oh hurry up, get some, there’s a whole cart outside…
lines.stage_direction: entering, carrying an armful of those little soaps. Sees the Walthams and stops.

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: Goodbye Geller.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Now, hold on! Hold on! Look, look, your daughter and I are supposed to leave tonight for our honeymoon, now-now you-you tell her that I’m gonna be at that airport and I hope that she’ll be there too! Oh yeah, I said Rachel’s name, but it didn’t mean anything, Okay? She’s-she’s just a friend and that’s all! That’s all! Now just tell Emily that I love her and that I can’t imagine spending my life with anyone else. Please, promise me that you’ll tell her that.
lines.stage_direction: Stops him Rachel sits down, depressed.

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: All right, I’ll tell her. Come on bugger face!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
lines.text: Call me.
lines.stage_direction: As she walks pass Ross, she pats his but.

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: You spend half your life in the bathroom, why don’t you ever go out the bloody window!

scenes.scene_description: They both try to slowly extricate themselves from Ross, but there’s a knock on the door that awakens him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y’know, maybe it’s best that we never got to do it again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, it kinda makes that-that one night special. Y’know, technically we still are over international waters.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’m gonna go to the bathroom, maybe I’ll see you there in a bit?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: ‘Kay!

scenes.scene_description: A 747 somewhere over the North Atlantic, Monica and Chandler are sitting in first class, depressed.

scenes.location: A 747 somewhere over the North Atlantic

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Can I ask you something?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uhh, no.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Felicity and I, we’re watching My Giant , and I was thinking, "I’m never gonna be as good an actor as that giant." Do you think I’m just wasting my life with this acting thing?

lines.text: No.

lines.text: I mean, the giant is like five years younger than me, y’know, you think I’ll ever get there?

lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks man.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay man.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But what about how much taller he is than me?

scenes.scene_description: Monica gets up and heads for the bathroom, Chandler turns to watch her go and is startled to see Joey sitting in Monica’s seat.

scenes.location: A 747 somewhere over the North Atlantic

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I mean, there’s no way I can make myself taller now, y’know? And who knows what science will come up with in the future, but Chandler, what if I die an unsuccessful, regular sized man?

scenes.scene_description: Time lapse, Chandler is finishing his third little bottle of booze.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, Monica, wow you’ve been in the bathroom for like a half-hour.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Had the beef-tips, huh?

lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi!

lines.text: You ate meat! You had sex!

lines.text: No we didn’t!

lines.text: I know you didn’t, I was talking about Monica.

lines.text: Phoebe, I did not have sex.

lines.text: All right, I’m gonna go say hi the chick and the duck.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh right, maybe I’ll just go home.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, we certainly are alone.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes! Good thing we have that, ‘Not in New York’ rule.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Right. Umm, listen since we’re-we-re on that subject, umm, I just wanted to tell you that uh, well, I-I was going through a really hard time in London, what with my brother getting married and that guy thinking I was Ross’s mother…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, an-anyway, I just—that night meant a lot to me, I guess I’m just trying to say thanks.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh. Y’know, that night meant a lot to me too, and it wasn’t because I was in a bad place or anything, it just meant a lot to me ‘cause, you’re really hot! Is that okay?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And I’m cute too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And you’re cute too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thank you! All right, I gotta go unpack.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bye.

lines.text: I’m still on London time, does that count?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That counts!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, good!

lines.text: Hi!

lines.text: Oh.

lines.text: Ohh, so no sign of Emily huh?

lines.text: Yeah, I do. I think you should go, by yourself, get some distance, clear your head, I think it’d be really good.

lines.text: Oh, I don’t, I don’t, I don’t know…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, come on Ross! I think it would be really good for you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I could, yeah, I can do that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can’t, I can’t even believe her! No, y’know what, I am, I am gonna go!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Right!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Couldn't you just stay like this forever? Chandler! Couldn't you just stay here forever?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, here, somewhere else, y'know where-where ever.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I'm cool. Casual.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What-what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm just hanging out. Y'know, having fun. Y'know with the girl that I'm seeing casually.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Man, I knew it! I knew you were going to do this!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Get all freaked out because everybody was talking and just joking around about marriage and stuff.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you do want all that stuff, right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh and you know what I want!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes! You want babies! You have baby fever!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I do not have baby fever!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh please, you are obsessed with babies and-and marriage and everything that's related to babies and-and marriage! I've got an idea, why don't we turn down the heat on this pressure cooker?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Have you lost your mind? Chandler, this isn't about me! This is about you and all your weird relationship commitment crap!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nah-uh! I know you! Okay? I know the thoughts that you have in the head--in your head!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You don't know everything. Did you know that I'm going out with Rachel tonight instead of you? Hmm? And did you know that the only baby around here is you?! And did you know that I can't even look at you right now?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I did not know that.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's, Chandler and Monica are curled up on one of the chairs.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's gonna be okay, right? I mean she's not gonna leave me? This is, this is fixable.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: By me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, no!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, unless you make some kind of big gesture.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, big!

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Chandler is getting advice from Ross and Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh-oh, shht! The Misses.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Gunther, can I get a coffee to go?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm still not done not wanting to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Just tell me what I need to do to make things right.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, that's what we do. Y'know, I-I mess up and then you tell me how to fix it and then I do and then y'know you think I'm all cute again.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really? I'm really tired of being your relationship tutor. You're gonna have to figure this one for yourself. All right? Y'know what? If you're too afraid to be in a real relationship, then don't be in one.

Chandler turns to watch her go and then sees Ross and Joey both with huge grimaces on their faces.

lines.text: Phoebe!

lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.text: Oh, I thought this was your party and it turns out it’s a party for Howard. He's just the sweetest little man!

lines.text: Yeah, but they didn't ask me! Y'know? This way I'm just y'know, the exotic, generous stranger. That's always fun to be.

lines.text: Yeah, but you're making me look bad!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No I'm not. No! If anything I'm making you look better! They'll see you talking to me and that's--I'm a hit!

lines.text: Hey!

So he takes it off and puts on one that says Dr. Geller and he puts the Ross one underneath the Dr. Geller one. Then as he turns off the music, we hear the party for Howard raging in the apartment across the hall.

lines.text: Yeah, okay, my bad.

lines.text: Hi!

lines.text: Ohh, you're so sweet!

lines.text: Huh.

lines.text: Uhh….

lines.text: All right, everyone calm down! Everyone calm down! I have something that I would like to say! Who here likes Ross? Of course you don't like him! I-I'll be honest with you guys, when I first met Ross I didn't like him at all! But then once I got to know him I saw that he's really sweet and caring and very generous. I mean, all I'm saying is don't judge Ross before you get to know him all right? I mean, I like all you guys now, but when I first meet you y'know Kurt, I thought, y'know abrasive drunk, umm Lola, mind numbingly stupid! And okay, you guys Gold-digger, cradle robbing perv! So, I think you all know what I mean.

lines.text: Hi!

lines.text: Hm-mmm.

title: The One With The Ride Along

lines.text: Okay, guys, listen, don't forget that tomorrow is the day that Emily gets married again, so whatever we do, just try to really keep Ross's mind off of it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, yeah, good idea.

lines.text: Hey man, what's up?

lines.text: Oh, eh, just thinking about Emily getting married tomorrow.

lines.text: Hey!

lines.text: Hey!

lines.text: How are you?

lines.text: Uh-huh!

lines.text: I want to go on a ride along!

lines.text: Me too!

lines.text: Okay!

lines.text: Yeah, yeah! Me too!

lines.text: Really?! You?

lines.text: Yeah.

lines.text: Hey!

lines.text: Okay, I've broken them down into categories. Okay, we have uh, we got holidays, birthdays, candids, y'know… And then what I've done is I've cross-referenced them by subject. Right? So if you're looking up, oh let's say birthdays and dogs, you get Photo 152. See?

lines.text: Ohh, it's me and La Poo! Wow! I miss that dog.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You can also find him under umm, dog and dead.

lines.text: Great! Thanks!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, hand me that other box of photos; that's the very last one.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oops. Sorry! Well, good thing you number all of them, huh?

lines.text: I hadn't! Photo 152 was a prototype.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohhhh. Honey, honey, honey, it's okay, it's okay honey. I'm gonna fix you a drink, huh? Maybe a margarita?

lines.text: No honey, it's okay! Listen, I'll got to Ross's and get the blender, you get all the margarita stuff ready.

lines.text: All right, he's keys are in the drawer. Y'know what? I also need some cash.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, you want me to stop at the ATM?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nah, while you're at Ross's if you see any lying around…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! I-I-I don't, I don't do that!

scenes.scene_description: She picks up the box and the bottom falls out, spilling all of the pictures onto Monica's neat little piles.
Rachel slowly leaves the apartment.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That was so cool man, the way you leaned on that guy.

lines.text: It is starting to get dark out there.

lines.text: He told you everything! I mean you totally cracked him!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Yeah well, being that he was the victim, they're usually pretty talkative.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. But it is officially nighttime.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh hey, Gary, want me to grab the berry for ya?

lines.text: It's called the cherry.

lines.text: It's the—Chandler!!

lines.text: Okay, I got it! This place makes the best sandwich in the world!

lines.text: Oh-ho, yeah!

lines.text: Yep!

lines.text: Yeah, okay.

lines.text: Did I say you could smell it?!

lines.text: Okay, I'll give them back. Look! What is so great about that sandwich?
lines.stage_direction: Exhales strongly through his nose and Joey just glares at him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, imagine the best sex you've ever had.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you thinking about Monica?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, what's that like?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh please don't be from a real dinosaur! Please! Please! Please! Please! Please! Please! Please! Please! Made in Mexico! Yes!! Ugh, who would buy this?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rach! It's me! Pick up!

scenes.scene_description: Ross's apartment, Rachel is just entering. She takes off her coat and heads for the kitchen. As she does, she knocks something off of a bookcase next to the kitchen door with her coat and it breaks.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! What's up?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I need a few more things to make the margaritas. Uhh, I need some salt, some margarita mix, and tequila.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So all we have is ice?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: See if he has ice.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Hello Ross? It's Emily. I know this is out of the blue but uh, I'm getting married tomorrow. Well, maybe I am. I keep thinking about you and I'm wondering if-if we made a mistake giving up so fast. Are you thinking about me? Of course you're not, but if you are, call me tonight. Okay, bye.
lines.stage_direction: on answering machine Rachel runs back into the room with the tequila.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. Oh!

scenes.scene_description: She hangs up the phone and starts to head back to the kitchen and notices some money lying out, stops, reaches down to pick it up, the phone rings causing her to drop it, she quickly puts it back, and heads for the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So where are we going next?

lines.text: This witness won't return my calls so we're gonna see if we can surprise him coming home.

lines.text: Sur-surprise him? We're not, we're not gonna make anybody mad are we?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on man! Listen so uh, are you gonna squeeze the perps shoes a little bit before he lawyers up?

lines.text: It's a witness not a perp. And no one talks like that!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, no one talks like that!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh what? Like your Mr. Cop!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, I'm more cop than you two!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How do you figure that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hello! I'm in the front seat, okay? I'm Gary's partner!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, when you say partner it doesn't sound cop. It, it sounds gay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm, jealous!
lines.stage_direction: He drops the cherry and it turns on.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Hey, do you mind? We're under cover here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, no problem.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sorry! Sorry! Oh, there! Hey Gary, who am I? Phone home!
lines.stage_direction: He sticks it under his shirt It's just there flashing through his shirt

scenes.scene_description: The ride along.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look at Officer Ross riding back here with the visitors.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, what's up with that Serpico?

scenes.scene_description: Gary just glares at him.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Are you thinking about me? Of course you're not, but if you are, call me tonight. Okay, bye.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nooo!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well thank God you were here! I mean, we have to erase that!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! We can't do that!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We have too! I mean what if Ross's hears that and then calls her back and then they get back together? Is that what you want? Ross back with that controlling, neurotic, crazy Emily? The Emily that wouldn't let him see you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Noo! Oh no! No! God no! He should not get back together with her. I know that! You know that! Even Ross knows that! But that still doesn’t give us the right to erase his message!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm his sister, okay? I love him! I don't want to see him get hurt! Come on! Doesn’t that give me the right to control him—help him?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't think he's the one who needs help.

scenes.scene_description: Ross's apartment, Rachel is playing Emily's message to Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, look, she's obviously unstable, okay? I mean she's thinking about running out on her wedding day. Okay, fine! But I mean, look at the position she's putting him in! What's he gonna do? Ross is gonna run over there on the wedding day and break up the marriage?! I mean, who would do that?! Okay, fine, all right, but that's y'know, it's different! Although it did involve a lot of the same people.
lines.stage_direction: Rachel slowly turns and glares at her. Realizing what she just said. Rachel again turns and glares at her in disgust.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know what, this is obviously some kind of twisted joke she's trying to play on him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, you are crazy! I'm sorry, but she sounded generally upset! I mean, listen!
lines.stage_direction: She hits a button on the machine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Noooooooo!

scenes.scene_description: They both sit down.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, y'know, we-we're safe right? I mean nothing bad can go down !

lines.text: No. But that reminds me, sign this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Oh it's nothing, it just says that you can't sue the city if you scrap your knee or y'know, get your head blown off.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, hurry up. I want to sign that.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Okay, here he comes. What is he doing? What the hell is he doing?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? What? What? What is it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Is everything okay?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Okay, he sees us. Now don't move. Don't look at him.

scenes.scene_description: The ride along, they're all waiting outside of the witness's house and still in the car in the same places as before.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Hey, it's okay. It was just a car backfire. Hey, look at that! You tried to save your buddy. You see that? You see what he did?
lines.stage_direction: Joey slowly moves off of Ross.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You okay man?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh-huh. Thank Joey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh, HELLO !!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, I guess we should go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, wait. Wait.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh yeah right!
lines.stage_direction: She grabs the money and shoves into her pocket.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, Monica! Monica! We have to fix this!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: There's nothing we can do. You erased the message!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah well unless we tell him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, if you're gonna be totally rational about this, I can't argue with you! All right? Fine, if you wanna tell him, tell him. I just don't want to be a part of it.

scenes.scene_description: Ross's apartment, Monica has all the supplies she needs and is getting ready to leave.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, maybe that's Emily calling back to leave the exact same message.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Ross! It's you! I just want you to remember this feeling. You are lucky to be alive! So live everyday to the fullest. Love yourself, okay? Okay. Oh, and also get stamps. Bye!
lines.stage_direction: on the machine He hangs up.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! Play that message for Emily and this whole problem goes away!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Right?

scenes.scene_description: The phone rings.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Hey, anybody want to meet a hero?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: John Glenn is here?!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: No, Joey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pheebs, we had the most incredible night! Okay, so, we're in the car…

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Wait! Hold on! Hi!
lines.stage_direction: He goes over to Phoebe and gives her a kiss. To Phoebe

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Okay, go ahead.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, okay, so we're in the car. Right? And bang ! A shot was fired. And Joey with no regard for his own safety throws himself on me!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: My God, Joey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It was a car backfire!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, but-but he didn't know that!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I didn't know that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And it could've just as easily have been a bullet.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Hey Joe, you ever think about joining the force? We could use a guy like you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Who jumps at loud noises!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! I could've died tonight.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah! If the car that backfired had run over you! Y'know what, I think I'll go home before Ross starts rambling about his newfound respect for life.
lines.stage_direction: He gets up and starts for the door.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I do have a newfound respect for life .

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: So you wanna get some dinner?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! Sure! Yep! Oh, y'know what? If I heard a shot right now, I'd throw my body on you.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Oh yeah? Well maybe you and I should take a walk through a bad neighborhood.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Bye!

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, the guys are returning from the ride along to find Phoebe already there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Cut it out Ross! I hate to have to save your life and kick your ass in the same day!

scenes.scene_description: They leave as Ross stares in awe at Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude! How come you took off?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I just went for a walk, around the living room. Whatever…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Is something wrong?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. No I'm just tired. Y'know, from-from the walk.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You dove in front of Ross! Ross!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's what this is about! Oh my God, you hate Ross!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I do not hate Ross!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Of course you do! I saved him! You're mad at me! It all adds up! You want Ross out of the picture.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What picture?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know, but I don't like what I'm hearing!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look I'm very glad that you saved Ross from the car backfire, but y'know, it could've been a bullet and you y'know, you didn't try to save me!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohh, you're upset because you think I chose Ross over you! No! I…knew…you could take care of yourself. Y'know, I mean Ross, he need help. He's not street like us!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: When it comes down to it, you would risk your life for Ross before you would for me. That's the bottom line.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, no, not exactly! All right, look, I, I wasn't trying to save Ross. Okay? My sandwich was next to Ross. All right? I was, I was trying to save my sandwich.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: From a bullet!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know it doesn't make much sense…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Much sense?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look Chandler, it was instinct! Okay? I just went for it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So you risked your life, for a sandwich!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know it sounds crazy, but Chandler this is the greatest sandwich in the world!
lines.stage_direction: Goes and picks up the sandwich

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So you didn't uh, choose Ross before me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! I would never do that! You-you're like my brother!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! In fact, to prove how much you mean to me, here.
lines.stage_direction: He unwraps the sandwich and holds it out for Chandler

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, eh, oh-oi, easy, it's not a hot dog!

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's, Chandler is pacing as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How good is that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh-oi-ho, yeah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: See?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hm-hmm.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-whoa-hey, dude, what are you doing?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I thought you were showing me how much you mean to me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. With a bite! Gee-e-e-eez!

scenes.scene_description: Chandler takes a bite.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, what uh, what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! Y'know what? You are in our apartment all the time! Okay? This is, this is just a drop in the bucket mister!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know, it-it doesn't matter. The important thing is that you're here. You're my friend, and you're here. Oh!
lines.stage_direction: He goes over and hugs her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, just a little scared. What's going on Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The most amazing thing happened tonight. I thought my number was up. I had an actual near death experience!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! What? What happened?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, okay, we were on the ride along with Gary, right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And somebody took a shot at me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, a car backfired, but I thought somebody was taking a shot at me. And Rach, I…I survived! And I was filled with this-this great respect for life. Y'know? I-I want to experience every moment. I want to seize every opportunity. I-I am seeing everything so-so clearly now.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because a car backfired?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, why are you here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I-I-I don't know how this fits into your whole "seizing" thing but um, Emily called you today.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You talked to her?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, she left a message. But it-it kinda got erased. There's just something wrong with your machine.
lines.stage_direction: He starts for the machine. Stopping him. Pause

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, okay, what-what did she say?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, uh something about having second thoughts about the wedding and did you guys make a mistake breaking up and uh, she wants you to call her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Now, that-that was a good thing that I told you, right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh? Yeah! Yes, of course!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Thank you! Thank you! Because—I'm sorry, all right. Because y'know what? She didn't want me—not important. The point is, I was right. Your decision. Okay? I was right. Your decision.
lines.stage_direction: She starts for the door. Stops

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right. I guess, I guess I should call Emily.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, no, that's not the right decision. That's not, that's not right, no Ross-Ross, come on! I mean, that woman made you miserable! Okay, Ross, do you really want to get back into that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, look, yesterday I would've even considered calling her back, but my ex-wife calls on the same day I have a near death experience. I mean, that-that has got to mean something!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh, Ross! That was not a near death experience! That was barely an experience!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You weren't there! Okay, maybe this is something that I-I'm supposed to seize! Y'know?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, y'know what? Maybe, this is not about seizing stuff. Maybe this is about escaping stuff.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I mean, look-look today you escaped death, y'know? And maybe this is a chance for you to escape getting back together with Emily?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That does make sense. Because I do wanna seize some opportunity, but I-I really don't wanna see or talk to her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, there you go!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Maybe today is just, close call day.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Close call day.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, thanks Rach.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, honey no problem. Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh wait-wait-wait! The message is blinking. Maybe you didn't erase it.

lines.text: Oh, you don't want me on the trip?

lines.speaker: Joey

lines.speaker: Chandler

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! I don't want you on the trip!

lines.speaker: Chandler

lines.text: Fine! Get out!

lines.text: Get out!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right!! Wait! Wait, there's no sidewalk! Yeah, I'm gonna die here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh-ho-ho!
lines.stage_direction: throws down a small wad of money, and as his hand twin starts to unfold it, Joey once again brings attention to their special gift to the world. {Y'know, looking at it now, they really don't have that similar of hands. Joey's are bigger.}

scenes.scene_description: A blackjack table, Joey is moving in to try and get his hand twin (who's dealing) to join him in his evil plot to rule the world! "Join me, and together we'll rule the universe as father and son!" (Sorry, I had a little Star Wars creep in there—Ooh, I have a big spoiler for The Phantom Menace , Yoda lives at the end! Ha-ha, spoiled it! Now you don't have to see it!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hello. My name is Regina Phalange. I'm a businesswoman in town on business. Would you like to see my card? Ooh, what did I do with my file-a-facts? I must've left it in conference room B.
lines.stage_direction: entering and sitting down at the table. Looks down

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hit me!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! May I just say that you two gentlemen have the exact same hands! They're identical! Now, I've never seen anything like that in the business world.

lines.speaker: Joey's Hand Twin
lines.text: Stop it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh, Ms. Phalange, may I ask you a question as an impartial person at-at this table?

lines.speaker: Joey's Hand Twin
lines.text: Please stop it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wouldn't you pay good money to see these identical hands showcased in some type of a uh, entertainment venue?

lines.speaker: Joey's Hand Twin
lines.text: If you leave now, I will chop off my hand and give it too you!

scenes.scene_description: The dealer stares at him and he stops.

lines.speaker: The Security Guard
lines.text: Didn't I just throw you out of here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, you threw out Phoebe. I'm Ms. Regina Phalange. Phalange!

lines.speaker: The Security Guard
lines.text: Come on, lady!

lines.speaker: Joey's Hand Twin
lines.text: Please, please take him too.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Me?! Oh come on, man! You can't do this! Come on! I'm your hand twin!!
lines.stage_direction: Being dragged out by the guard

scenes.scene_description: The security guard from before approaches and Phoebe tries to turn her back on him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hello! One marriage please!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yep, we wanna get married!

lines.speaker: The Attendant
lines.text: Well, there's a service in progress. Have a seat.

scenes.scene_description: A Little White Chapel , Chandler and Monica are entering.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Dum! Dum-dum-dum! Dum! Dum! Dum! Dum-dum-dum!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, that's The Wedding March . Does, does that freak you out?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, only because that's the graduation song.

scenes.scene_description: They both sit down.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay! This is it! We're gonna get married!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you sure you wanna do this?

scenes.scene_description: The real Wedding March begins playing from behind the closed doors of the chapel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, hello, Mrs. Ross!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, hello, Mr. Rachel!

scenes.scene_description: Suddenly the doors burst open, and ROSS AND RACHEL COME OUT ARM-IN-ARM!!!!! And Rachel's carrying a bouquet!!! THEY GOT MARRIED!!!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait! Okay!

scenes.scene_description: They storm out into the street.
